title: NPC Secrets
description: Something this NPC is concealing — a hidden truth that shapes how they behave.

entries.result: They did not earn their current position honestly. Someone who knew the truth is dead. They're not sure they killed them.
They are not who they say they are. The real version of this identity died years ago, and they stepped into it.
They have a child they've never met, somewhere, grown now. They've tracked them at a distance for years but never made contact.
They are terminally ill. They have told no one. They are trying to finish something before it ends.
They were responsible for a disaster that was attributed to someone else. That someone else suffered for it. They let it happen.
They are in significant debt to someone dangerous. The cheerful surface is a performance to buy time.
They betrayed a friend at a critical moment. The friend doesn't know it was them. The friendship is ongoing.
They are being blackmailed. They have been for years. The payments have been manageable until recently.
They do not believe what they publicly claim to believe. They have believed the opposite for a long time and cannot afford to say so.
They stole something significant from their employer, community, or family. The theft has never been discovered. They still have it.
They survived something by leaving others behind. The official story is different. The survivors who know the truth are few.
They have been reporting on the people around them to a third party. It started as practical necessity. It has continued.
They are in love with someone they absolutely should not be in love with, and have been managing this for longer than is probably healthy.
They killed someone. It was not in combat, it was not in clear self-defense, and they have never told anyone.
Their family does not know they're alive. They chose to disappear. They sometimes wonder if anyone looked.
They have been lying about their abilities or qualifications for years. So far, the gap between claim and reality hasn't been fatal.
They are addicted to something, have been for years, and are better at hiding it than at stopping.
They know where something very valuable is hidden. They have never retrieved it. They are not sure what they're waiting for.
They are not as loyal as they appear. They have already decided which side they'll take when the choice becomes unavoidable.
They sabotaged something important once — a relationship, a mission, a career — and the person it hurt never found out why it failed.
They witnessed a crime and said nothing. The wrong person was punished. They have lived with this.
They have a second family — a partner, children — in another place. Neither family knows about the other.
They are far wealthier than they let on. The modesty is deliberate, maintained for reasons that still make sense to them.
They are far poorer than they let on. The appearance of stability is costing them everything they have left.
They are in contact with someone everyone else believes is dead. They have reasons for keeping the contact secret.
They did not write the work they are credited for. They have built a reputation on someone else's ideas.
They have a specific phobia so severe it has dictated major life decisions, and they have concealed it completely.
They have been feeding information to the opposition — not for ideology, but for money, and the money has been spent.
They are the reason a specific person is dead. It was an accident. They chose silence over confession.
They have been planning to leave — the job, the city, the relationship — for longer than they've admitted. They have already made some of the arrangements.
They don't actually remember a significant portion of a night that others reference warmly. Something happened. They don't know what.
They are not the person who performed the act they are celebrated for. They accepted the credit by default and have never corrected the record.
They have a chronic condition that affects their judgment at certain intervals, and they are very careful about when those intervals fall.
They owe their current position to a favor from someone they despise. The favor hangs over everything.
They read someone's private correspondence once, years ago, and know something about them that was never meant to be known.
They have been stealing from the communal funds in small amounts for a long time. The cumulative amount is no longer small.
They were the informant who exposed a ring of wrongdoers. They were also a member of that ring.
They made a promise in extremity that they never intend to keep, to someone who still believes they will.
They have never been to the place they claim to be from. The accent is practiced. The references are researched.
They are dying slowly of something curable that they refuse to treat, for reasons that make sense to them.
They are in contact with someone who most people in their life consider dangerous or forbidden — family, enemy, lover.
They were once arrested and the record was expunged. The act itself was not erased.
They have been writing down everything. A journal, thorough and honest. If it were found, several things would unravel.
They know something happened to them that they cannot account for — a gap in memory, an inexplicable wound, a period of days they cannot locate.
They have been protecting someone who does not deserve protection, and they know it, and they are not ready to stop.
They sold something that wasn't theirs to sell. The original owner is still looking for it.
They have a habit they're ashamed of — not dangerous, just embarrassing — that they've kept hidden from everyone who knows them.
They had a complete personal breakdown some years back. They put themselves back together. No one who knows them now knows what they looked like before.
They are the source of a rumor they publicly deny spreading. The rumor was true. They knew it was true when they shared it.
They once had a chance to stop something terrible from happening and did nothing. The decision took about two seconds.
They are not as good at what they do as their reputation suggests. So far, the people who've noticed have not survived to report it.
They have been lying about where they go on a particular recurring interval — every week, every month. The truth is mundane. The lying is habit now.
They forged a document early in their career that they are still benefiting from. The forgery was professional.
They are terrified of one specific person and have organized their life around never being alone with them.
They have been offered a way out of their current situation and have not taken it, for reasons they haven't examined.
They don't know who their parent is. They've been told a story that is almost certainly false and have never pursued the truth.
They have done something that, if the people who love them knew, they would be loved less. This is the thing they carry most.
They have been saving money from their income for years into an account no one knows about. It is enough to disappear.
They gave testimony that put someone away. Parts of the testimony were not entirely accurate. They told themselves it didn't matter.
They are in the early stages of something — a romance, a conspiracy, a betrayal, a departure — that has not yet become visible.
They have been receiving anonymous letters that contain accurate information about things they've done. They don't know who is sending them.
They survived by turning on the group they were part of. The remaining members think someone else was responsible.
They have a professional relationship with someone they are simultaneously sabotaging — slowly, carefully, deniably.
They know the location of a body. It is not their fault. They have never told anyone because the explanation is complicated.
They are in possession of information that would significantly change someone else's life. They have held it for years, uncertain whether releasing it would help or hurt.
They rejected someone who later died, and they have never stopped wondering if they could have changed the ending.
They are secretly in contact with the person everyone believes wronged them. The actual story is more complicated.
They feel nothing in circumstances where everyone around them clearly feels something. They have become very good at performing the appropriate emotion.
They have a second identity — documentation, contacts, a history — that they have been maintaining against the day they need it.
They gave up someone who trusted them, not out of malice but out of fear, and neither of those words has ever been said out loud.
They have an ongoing correspondence with someone from a part of their life they claim to have severed completely.
They know their current good fortune is temporary — the mechanism is in place for it to collapse — and they have not warned the people who will be affected.
They were given a gift — money, an opportunity, an introduction — by someone they have publicly distanced themselves from.
They have been sleeping very poorly for months. They have a very good reason. They have told no one.
They don't believe the cause they're publicly working for will succeed. They continue anyway for reasons they'd rather not examine.
They copied something — work, an idea, a design — from someone who could not defend their claim to it. The copy became everything.
They were present at something that officially did not happen. The official story required their silence and they gave it.
They are being watched. They know it. They don't know by whom.
They helped cover up an accident that became a crime in the covering. They told themselves it was loyalty.
They have been trying to contact someone who will not respond, for longer than is probably healthy, for reasons that still feel reasonable.
They don't know if the child they are raising is theirs. They have decided they don't need to know. Some days they are sure. Most days they are not.
They were responsible for a failure that was attributed to equipment, circumstance, or fate. They have accepted the attribution.
They are planning something that the people around them would try to stop if they knew.
They have a specific trigger — a word, a smell, a sound — that produces a response they cannot control and have gotten very good at excusing.
They paid for their current reputation. Literally. The circumstances of the payment were complicated.
They have been diagnosed with something they've told no one. They are not sure how much time they have and are acting accordingly in ways that aren't fully visible.
They were complicit in something that hurt someone they care about, and that person never found out they were involved.
They don't remember parts of their past clearly and have filled those gaps with a story that is largely constructed.
They have information about an ongoing injustice and have chosen to sit on it. The reasons still seem good, mostly.
They are not the first person to hold this identity. Someone used it before them. What happened to that person is not entirely clear.
They set a fire once — metaphorical or literal — and have spent years working in the reconstruction effort.
They tried to do the right thing once and it made things significantly worse. They have not tried that particular thing since.
They have been lying about something so small for so long that the lie has become load-bearing — the whole structure rests on it now.
They are the reason a relationship between two people they care about failed. Neither of those people knows.
They have a talent they've suppressed completely because it is inconvenient or dangerous to the life they are trying to live.
They accepted money once to say a specific thing about a specific person. They said it. They are not sure they were wrong, but they are sure they were paid.
They have been waiting for someone to ask them a specific question so they can finally answer it. No one has asked.
They know the ending of something everyone else is still in the middle of, and they've been deciding for months what to do with that knowledge.
They have already started over once, completely, leaving everything behind. What they left behind is still back there.
They are not afraid of the thing they tell people they are not afraid of. They are afraid of something else entirely, and have redirected the question for so long it has become automatic.
They lied to protect someone, and the lie has protected them more than it protected the person it was meant for.
They lost something they were entrusted with — important, irreplaceable — and have been substituting a copy ever since.
They have a recurring dream they've never told anyone about. The dream is specific and disturbing and directly relevant to their waking life.
They once abandoned someone who needed them. The person survived. They have not spoken since. They check on them from a distance.
They are the original author of something attributed to a name no one has ever been able to find.
They have been slowly, carefully destroying evidence of something for months, and they are nearly done.
They know their colleague, partner, or ally is compromised. They have not acted on this yet. Every day they don't act is a choice.
They have a physical mark — a brand, a tattoo, a scar — that they keep covered and that would identify a past affiliation they deny.
They confided in someone once, completely, and that person used it against them. They have never confided fully in anyone since.
They know where the money went. They have not said so. They are not sure who they are protecting.
They have had the same opportunity to do the thing they claim to want twice now, and turned it down both times.
They fell in love once, deeply, with someone who did not feel the same, and have been making decisions in reaction to that ever since.
They have been lying about their age — older or younger — for so long it has replaced the real number in their own head.
They have a key to a place they no longer have reason to have a key to, and they have kept it.
They have been reading someone's correspondence without permission. It started as a precaution and has become compulsive.
They know that the story everyone believes about a particular event is wrong. They were there. They were the reason.
They don't feel remorse about the thing they should feel remorse about. They feel something, but it isn't that.
They are the reason their predecessor was removed from their position. It wasn't a conspiracy — just an observation made to the right person at the right time.
They have a plan for a very specific outcome that they have shared with no one, that they have been working toward for years.
They once made a deal that seemed reasonable at the time. The other party has not forgotten it. They are waiting to see when it will be called in.
They are the biological parent of someone they know only as an acquaintance.
They have never actually completed the training or qualification they are assumed to have.
They are deeply, privately religious in a way that would surprise everyone who thinks they know them.
They are deeply, privately irreligious in a way that would cause significant problems if known.
They have been contacted by someone from their past who has resurfaced at a very inconvenient time.
They once had a complete identity that they shed like a skin — name, history, everything — and began again.
They have been sending money anonymously to someone they wronged, and will keep doing so until the debt feels balanced, which it probably never will.
They know the truth about how a famous or important event actually unfolded, and the truth is not flattering to the accepted history.
They have a copy of something very sensitive — correspondence, records, evidence — and they have not decided whether to use it or destroy it.
They have been replaced — in someone's affections, in a professional role, in a family structure — and have never acknowledged it out loud.
They are the last person who knows a specific piece of information, and when they die it will be lost, and they are increasingly uncertain whether that is a tragedy.
They were not where they said they were when something important happened. The alibi is false. The thing they were actually doing is separate.
They found something that wasn't meant for them to find — a document, an object, a scene — and have been deciding what to do with the knowledge ever since.
They are not recovering as well as they have led others to believe. They present progress. The interior is still rubble.
They were given a second chance by someone who didn't know they were giving it, and they have tried to deserve it ever since.
They have been faking an aspect of their personality — warmth, confidence, expertise — for so long that the line between performance and truth is no longer clear.
They know they are being lied to by someone they depend on, and have not confronted it because they don't know what they would do with the truth.
They destroyed a relationship between two others deliberately, for reasons that seemed good at the time and seem less good on reflection.
They know something is wrong with a plan everyone else has accepted. They have said nothing because the time to say something has passed.
They are not coping. The surface is well-maintained. The interior is a different situation entirely.
They took credit for an act of generosity performed by someone else, and the good reputation they have built from it is partly borrowed.
They have never forgiven someone they publicly reconciled with, and perform the warmth of forgiveness with considerable skill.
They have held a piece of information back from everyone they trust because they don't yet trust anyone with all of it.
They have relapsed after a period of public recovery and are managing the concealment with the same effort they once put into the recovery.
They are the reason something went wrong on a night that changed several people's lives, and no one has ever looked closely enough to see it.
They know where someone went who everyone thinks disappeared. They have kept this to themselves because being asked would require explaining how they know.
They have been trying to work up the courage to do something for years. They are getting closer. The people around them are about to be surprised.
They stole an idea — not the whole thing, just enough of it — and the original thinker never had the standing to be believed if they tried to claim it.
They know their good name is partially constructed. The events it rests on were real, but the interpretation of them was carefully shaped.
They let someone believe something false about themselves, not by lying but by never correcting a misunderstanding that flatters them.
They have been trying to end a specific relationship for a long time and have not found a way to do it that doesn't cause harm.
They carry a wound that is still healing — physically or otherwise — that they would rather no one know about.
They have already decided what they will do if the situation deteriorates in the way they expect it to. None of the people around them have been included in that plan.
They loved someone whose name they never say, who changed them fundamentally, who is part of every decision they make.
They are maintaining a relationship out of obligation that they would end immediately if they thought they could.
They have made a significant financial or personal commitment based on a future that may not arrive, and they are not sure how to account for the gap if it doesn't.
They once broke the law in a way that felt justified at the time and still does, mostly.
They know their most trusted ally has been withholding something. They haven't asked because they don't want to force the issue.
They are responsible for someone else being blamed for a thing they did, and the blamed party never found out. The blamed party is still in their life.
They have told different people incompatible versions of the same important story and live with the knowledge that the versions could eventually be compared.
They have a child they gave up and have never told anyone about.
They once failed someone at a moment that mattered, chose their own safety over another's, and constructed a narrative that partially absolves them.
They want something they've decided they're not allowed to want, and the wanting has not diminished with the deciding.
They have been approached with a very interesting offer and have not yet said no.
They made a decision during a crisis that cannot be undone, that no one witnessed, that was probably wrong.
They have not been honest with a person they are very close to about a fundamental aspect of who they are.
They were there when something ended and did nothing to stop it because they were not sure they wanted to.
They owe their life to someone they despise, and the gratitude and the contempt have been living in them side by side for years.
They know that the version of events that exonerates them is only one plausible interpretation of what happened.
They have been lying about being fine, specifically and only to the person most likely to be able to help them.
They are the one who made the first move — the first act that set a chain of consequences in motion — and have never claimed it.
They have an exit prepared — packed, positioned, funded — and use it as a psychological support rather than an actual plan to leave. Or so they tell themselves.
They once spent a significant amount of time in a situation — captivity, isolation, an institution — that they have not disclosed to the people in their current life.
They know which of their peers is selling information. They have not reported it because they are not sure the people who would receive the report are clean.
They don't actually know the answer to the question they are considered an authority on. They answer around it and have for years.
They have been grieving something for so long that the grief has become infrastructure, invisible to everyone, load-bearing.
They made a promise to themselves at a low moment — a very specific promise about what they would do if they survived — and they are keeping it now.
They feel something profound for someone they are supposed to be neutral toward, and managing this takes more effort than anything else they do.
They have been using their position to protect someone who does not know they are being protected.
They were once very different from who they are now. The transition was not gradual. Something broke and was rebuilt as something else.
They know the right answer to a situation everyone else is agonizing over and are waiting to see if anyone arrives at it independently.
They have said something unforgivable to someone in a private moment. The someone has apparently forgiven it. They have not forgiven themselves.
They are holding a grudge that has softened over the years into something that looks a lot like old love, and they have not figured out what to do with that.
They are watching someone make the same mistakes they made, and have said nothing, either because they were not asked or because they don't think it would help.
They don't know why they survived when others didn't, and the question has not let them rest in years.
They have been testing the people around them — small tests, ongoing, for years — and most people have been failing.
They know something about their own origins that others would find significant and that they have decided to sit on indefinitely.
They once chose ambition over someone who loved them and are still making the calculation of whether it was worth it.
They have been pretending not to understand something they understand perfectly, because not understanding is more useful.
They are the source of the rumor that nearly destroyed someone. The rumor was mostly false. They knew that when they shared it.
They have been doing something quietly good — charitable, selfless, useful — that they have hidden from everyone, for reasons they haven't fully examined.
They know what the other party in a current negotiation or conflict actually wants, and they have not shared this because the knowledge is more useful kept.
They have never finished grieving something they lost when they were very young, and it surfaces in ways they don't always recognize.
They have been given access they were not meant to have — a room, a record, a relationship — and have been careful not to flag that they have it.
They know they are a suspect in something, and they did not do that particular thing, and they are not sure how to prove it without explaining where they actually were.
They have been watching someone they care about move toward a bad decision and have prepared exactly what they will say when asked, which they expect to happen soon.
They have rehearsed a specific conversation they need to have so many times that the rehearsed version has taken on more reality than what will actually occur.
They don't know if they did the right thing, and they have stopped expecting to ever know.
They carry the name of someone they failed with them, every day, in a way no one can see.
They are exactly who they say they are. Everything is exactly as it seems. This is somehow the most disorienting thing about them.
